entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud and Datasphere News in February
entry.content:
SAP Business Data Cloud and Datasphere News in February

February depression—no way!
Plenty of great features and enhancements being delivered with our two releases in SAP Datasphere and BDC, and SAP Analytics Cloud also delivered a packed quarterly release.

Learn more in my short top features video in February 2026 on YouTube 📺 as well as the QRC1 2026 release for SAP Analytics Cloud by @Orla_C .

In addition, explore the latest updates in our community news blogs and more.

 

My top features in February

 

Data Pipeline Analyzer

The Data Pipeline Analyzer is a monitoring tool designed for real-time analysis of complex data models, providing immediate visibility into errors, warnings, and data discrepancies. By focusing on node status indicators and streamlined navigation, the tool allows users to pinpoint the exact location of data transfer failures. This targeted troubleshooting approach ensures efficient oversight of flows, enabling organizations to maintain high data quality and resolve issues promptly to prevent downstream disruptions.

➡️  More information.

 

Task Chain Output Parameter

After introducing input parameters already in Q3 last year, you can now use output parameters in task chains. You can also map output parameters to the task chain from the task object. This will allow for more flexible usage of task chains and nested task chains.

➡️  More information.

 

Transformation Flow Object Versioning

The versioning concept you know for other modeling objects is now available for transformation flows. You can now review and restore versions, and the general options are similar to other editors where versioning is already available. You can:

  • Open a past version in read-only mode.
  • Download a past version to a CSN/JSON file.
  • Restore a past version to replace the current version.

➡️  More information.

 

Local Table (Files) Delete Inbound Buffer

Local Tables (File) store a large amount of data, including delta capture changes and historical data. When data is updated, the information is kept on your local table (file) with the delta changes columns, and a new version is stored in the object store. Keeping all these records and versions can consume a lot of memory, and can impact performance.
You can now delete records waiting in the inbound buffer of a local table (file), even if a merge task has not run.

➡️  More information.

 

Visibility of Data Access Controls Applied to Sources

A small but helpful feature, where you can now see data access controls applied to the sources of your view in a subsection Applied via Sources under the list of Data Access Controls in the side panel.

➡️  More information.

 

Sharing of Analytic Models

We all waited a long time for the cross-space sharing of an analytic model together with its contributing entities like the fact, dimensions, hierarchies, and text entities. You can share an analytic model with one or even with multiple spaces.

This enables users in the target space with the ability to stack on the model, inheriting its properties and enhancing the new model to meet their needs.

➡️  More information.

 

 

SAP Analytics Cloud Feature Highlights QRC1

The first release for SAP Analytics Cloud for 2026 is already out and @orla Cullen dives a little deeper into the top 5 features of the first quarter:

🔹New Chart Types – Expanded native visualizations with Area and Pareto charts to simplify advanced analysis.

🔹Story Versioning – Create, manage, and restore up to 10 major story versions for better iterative control.

🔹Comment Management – A new dashboard to search, filter, copy, and bulk-delete comments across models and versions.

🔹My Metrics Alerting – Subscribe to metric reports, schedule delivery, and get in-app/email notifications when metrics change.

🔹Live Connectivity to Snowflake – Extended external live data access for planning models connected directly to Snowflake.

 

 

 

Webinar Series: The Next Era of Business Data

SAP Business Data Cloud is part of a comprehensive strategy for enterprise data designed to address complex enterprise data management challenges. We provide an overview of SAP Business Data Cloud and guide you through its integral pillars and strategic vision.

👉 Sign up now: SAP BDC: The Next Era of Business Data

🔹Jan 29 | Discover SAP Business Data Cloud in 2026 - recording

🔹Feb 05 | The Smart Path to SAP BW Modernization - recording

entry.title: Re: email setting for billing form
entry.content:
Good day @jpriya 

Thank you for your question.

You cannot use C_BILLINGDOCUMENTOMPARAMDET if it is not releasee or extensible in your system. The clean core way is to bring your own released condition parameter into OPD and then use it in Output Relevance or Channel rules.

See KBA 3689607 and the following references provide more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/f8c835a5ad194e5eae0d676fdb88884e.html?locale=en-US

The Public Cloud allows you to add a custom field as an OPD condition parameter for billing output, then use it in OPD rules. You can also control send email vs do not send email through OPD Output Relevance or Channel. However, it is not possible to extend non released CDS view directly.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/0e905b7d843743469b51317c179e7516.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/d9cc17d1aa404aee9bf2d10599c9a8d1.html?locale=en-US

The recommended best practice is to:

  • Crate a custom header flag in billing output context, for example: YY1_SEND_EMAIL_FLG = X when TaxNumber4 = 0, else blank
  • Use OPD rule: if YY1_SEND_EMAIL_FLG= X à Output relevant = true and channel = Email. Else à Output relevant = false (this should not generate output item)

Here is a quick guidance in your system:

Open Custom Fields, create a new field (YY1_SEND_EMAIL_FLG) in the business context that is used for billing output and publish it.

You need to populate the flag from BP TaxNumber4. You need the flag value to be present at runtime of output determination. Use Key user custom logic to derive YY1_SEND_EMAIL_FLG to Sold To Party’s TaxNumber4 when the billing document output is created or populate a billing header custom field earlier in the process and reuse it in output.

Open Output Parameter Determination,

set Rules for Billing Document and set Determination Step to Output Relevance and create the rule as determined earlier.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Automating SAP HANA Cloud (Other Environment) DB Lifecycle Management with SAP Automation Pilot
entry.content:
In our recent updates around AI-enabled Ops Operations, we demonstrated how SAP Automation Pilot boosts structured, scalable, and intelligent automation for Day-2 Ops Agents through Joule.

Today, I am excited to take this one step further.

🚀  We are expanding our automation coverage to fully address SAP HANA Cloud lifecycle management in the Other Environment - bringing operational excellence to database management at scale.

From Standard Catalogs to Broader Lifecycle Automation

SAP Automation Pilot already provides a built-in catalog for managing SAP HANA Cloud database instances running in the Cloud Foundry environment - that is the the well-known SAP BTP Database Lifecycle Management (dblm-sapcp) Catalog.

This catalog has successfully helped customers automate essential lifecycle tasks directly within SAP BTP.

However, many customers also operate SAP HANA Cloud instances in the Other Environment, where lifecycle management is equally critical and operationally sensitive. While SAP Automation Pilot already offers all the technical capabilities required to automate those tasks, we recognized the need to package common scenarios into a structured, reusable, and publicly available catalog.

That is exactly what we have now delivered! 

Introducing: SAP HANA Cloud Lifecycle Management Sample Catalog (Other Environment)

We have consolidated a wide range of SAP HANA Cloud lifecycle scenarios into a dedicated public sample catalog, now available in our GitHub repository here: SAP HANA Cloud Lifecycle Management Sample Catalog (Other Environment)

This catalog provides a comprehensive and validated set of automation commands for managing SAP HANA Cloud instances at subaccount level using Service Manager.

Instead of executing repetitive and sensitive database operations manually, you can now automate domains such as: 🔹 Instance Lifecycle Management 🔹Backup & Recovery 🔹 Snapshot Management 🔹 Instance Configuration🔹 Upgrades & Plugins 🔹 Elastic Compute Nodes (ECN), etc. 

✅ All commands are validated and ready to be explored by our users - to help you minimize manual effort, reduce risk, and standardize operational procedures.

Beyond Automation: Toward Intelligent Database Operations

While structured lifecycle automation already delivers immediate value, the real transformation begins when automation becomes agentic.

Because all HANA Cloud-related commands are exposed through SAP Automation Pilot, they can be orchestrated, combined, and consumed in higher-level operational workflows. 

This naturally leads to the next evolution - AI Ops Agents for HANA Cloud enabled with SAP Automation Pilot and Joule! 

📖Be Part of the Journey - Join Our Customer Engagement Initiative

As we continue expanding agentic AI operations, collaboration with customers remains key.

If you would like to gain hands-on experience with AI-enabled Ops automation and help shape the future of intelligent database operations, we invite you to subscribe in our ongoing Customer Engagement Initiative: Agentic AI Operations with SAP Automation Pilot

Through this initiative, you can:

  • Explore real-world AI-driven automation scenarios;
  • Bring your own use cases and operational challenges;
  • Provide direct feedback to the product team;
  • Influence upcoming capabilities;

⏳ Registration is open until March 15, 2026 - secure your spot soon!

From structured lifecycle automation to fully agentic database operations - this is the next chapter of operational excellence with SAP Automation Pilot.

Let’s build it together!

entry.title: Re: Credit Memo Request to correct Debit Memo
entry.content:
Good day @Johannes_Wienho 

Thank you for your question and the details provided.

You can create a Credit Memo Request either without reference or with a reference to a sales order or a billing document.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/26f4ba4455c74d6eb882c0be0a36fe0c.html?locale=en-US

Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

If you cannot reference your L2 debit memo billing document, then you probably do not have the right authorisation, or you might be missing copy control from Billing Document to Sales Document.

  • You can resolve it in the Manage Credit Memo Request app by creating with Reference à Billing Document, then enter the L2 billing document number.
  • If you cannot select L2 as a reference, maintain copying control in CBC (use SSCUI and set Copying Control: Billing Document to Sales Document) for Source = L2 and Target = CR. See KBA 3269803 for further details.

After you have maintained copy control, retry creating the CR with reference and the system should copy the billing items from L2 into the Credit Memo Request.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Business role changes after upgrade in SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Good day @S-O 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, this kind of discrepancy can happen between Dev Test and Prod, especially around post upgrade IAM role adjustments and the new area Business Catalogs that supports IAM Apps.

In short, “Upgrade Change=Yes” is not a universal flag across all tenants. It reflects whether that specific tenant currently detects upgrade related deltas (for example role template differences, catalog deltas, restriction deltas) and whether those deltas have already been reviewed or applied in that tenant using the post upgrade tools.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/4d0c7453e8304942bcecb5bb5e6b72e7.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/2e2f201d17b346c480bcc2c5610fa0a7.html?locale=en-US

Here is what I suggest you do:

  • In each tenant open Managen Business Role Changes After Upgrade and download the CSV to compare the deleted deltas.
  • Open the impacted business role in Maintain Business Roles and compare assigned catalogs and restrictions line by line.
  • Because your change specifically references Business Catalogs, verify whether the catalogs in question appear under Business Catalogs Supporting IAM Apps and whether the set of catalogs differs across tenant.
  • If Dev upgrade is already applied, expect Test and Prod to show the same Upgrade Changes status only after their upgrade and post upgrade role review is executed.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/1068e29d58e34f59959b5dce108dfb8a.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/9bbbfc78b74a45af9059da66a149e507.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Does the API run SELECT statements in Custom Logic?
entry.content:
Hello @kienngo 

No, this is not because the batch request runs “faster”. This is almost always caused by lack of authorisation and CDS access control differences between your dialog user and the communication user. See KBA 3387665 for more details.

A batch request is not faster than select in a way that would cause sy-subrc = 4. The difference is usually the user context. When you manually create the record, the logic runs with you the authorisations of your dialog user. When you call the OData service, the logic runs with the communication user that is configured in the Communication Arrangement, and CDS access control may filter the CDS view results, so any Open SQL returns no rows and sy-subrc = 4

Basically, what is happening is that:

  • Your “manual creation” test runs under your interactive business user, which typically has broad authorisations, so the CDS views like I_CostCenter or user related views return data.
  • Your API call runs under a communication user that often has minimal authorisations.
  • CDS access control is applied implicitly for Open SQL reads of CDS entities, and if access control filters everything out, sy-subrc becomes 4 even if the record exists.

So, the record is not missing, it is just not available to that user.

You can resolve this in your system by:

  • First, identifying the inbound communication user in Communications Arrangement (Open the arrangement you use for your CBO OData service, in the Inbound Communication section note the username, which is the one Postman is effectively using)  

                      

  • Maintaining Business Users and assigning an appropriate business role that allows the user to read:
    • Cost center master data for the relevant controlling area
    • The user master entity you validate against 

  • You can then retest the same batch request.

This will ensure your validation logic remains clean and avoids bypassing security.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/494549178c074b25824412cb44cddec5.html?locale=en-US

 Alternatively, if your extensibility context allows it and you only need a technical existence check, you can use WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS for CDS views that have DCL, because they are necessary for some released CDS views. See KBA 3387665 for more details.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Date Control is not rendered properly in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Fixed: for some reason, the container z index has been set back to auto. Putting it back to 10 solved the issue.

entry.title: Understanding Common Git Flags and Options – Part I
entry.content:
Introduction:

Git is a powerful version control system that gives us fine-grained control over their workflow. Much of this power comes from flags and options that modify how Git commands behave.

In this two-part series, we will explore the most commonly used Git flags, explain what they do and show practical examples.

In Part I, we will focus on the essential, everyday flags we would likely use the most as.

Quick overview:

These following flags are part of our daily Git workflow:

  • -m → Add commit message
  • -a → Commit all tracked changes
  • -u → Set upstream branch
  • -v → Verbose output
  • -n → Dry run
  • -q → Quiet mode
  • -l → List references

1. -m (Commit Message)

Purpose: Add a commit message inline without opening an editor.

git commit -m "Fix login validation bug"

Without -m, Git opens your default editor to write the message. 

Please Note: This is one of the most frequently used Git flags.

 

 2. -a (All Tracked Changes)

Purpose: Automatically stage all modified tracked files before committing.

git commit -a -m "Update dashboard UI"

Please Note: This does not include new untracked files.

 

 3. -u (Set Upstream Branch)

Purpose: Set the upstream tracking branch.

git push -u origin feature-branch

After using this once, you can simply run: git push

Please Note: Git will automatically know which remote branch to push.

 

 4. -v (Verbose Output)

Purpose: Show more detailed output.

git push -v

Often used with:

git log
git commit
git push

Please Note: It is helpful for debugging &understanding what Git is doing behind the scenes.

 

 5. -n (Dry Run)

Purpose: Simulate a command without making changes.

git clean -n

This shows what would be removed without actually deleting anything.

Please Note: It is very useful before running destructive commands.

 

 6. -q (Quiet Mode)

Purpose: Suppress command output.

git status -q

Please Note: Commonly used in scripts or CI pipelines to reduce noise.

 

 7. -l (List)

Purpose: List references such as branches.

git branch -l

Please Note: It helps us to display all local branches.

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog!

If you found this helpful, feel free to share your thoughts, feedback, or questions in the comments! Let's keep learning and growing together. Happy coding! 

Hello experts, please feel free to correct me if any information is inaccurate.
